sections.heading: Role Mapping Process
sections.body:
- Atlas applies the role mappings when you log in.

- Atlas compares the IdP (Identity Provider) groups named **memberOf** to role
  mappings defined for your organizations. These organizations must
  use the same IdP (Identity Provider) that the user did to authenticate.

  > **Note:** If a user has role mappings from multiple IdP (Identity Provider) groups in an organization,
  > Atlas applies all roles that result from the user's group memberships
  > during login. This means the user gets all permissions from all their
  > mapped roles.

  - Atlas applies the mapped roles to federated users if you
    defined role mappings.

  - Atlas applies the default role if:

    - You don't have defined role mappings

    - Role mappings would result in a user without any roles

  - Organization role mappings define federated users' Atlas
    access. If a federated user logs in but doesn't belong to an IdP (Identity Provider)
    group mapped to a desired organization, Atlas removes the
    mapped role from the user in that organization and its projects.
    The federated user may still have other IdP (Identity Provider) groups.

    > **Example:** Consider a scenario where a user belongs to the **admin** IdP (Identity Provider)
    > group. You have configured a role mapping of **admin** to the
    > [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) in **Organization A**. If you
    > remove that user from the **admin** IdP (Identity Provider) group, Atlas
    > deletes that users' [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role when
    > the user next logs in.

  - Every organization must have at least one user that has the
    [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role. If removing a role removes
    the last owner from an organization, the removal fails.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To manage federated authentication, you must have
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access to one or more organizations that delegate
federation settings to the instance.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To complete this tutorial, you must have:

- Created an IdP (Identity Provider) application. This application must have a SAML
  attribute named to **memberOf**. Map this attribute to the IdP (Identity Provider)
  source attributes for groups. This attribute links the IdP (Identity Provider) groups
  with your Atlas roles.

- Linked an [IdP to Atlas](atlas-manage-fed-auth).

- Mapped an [Atlas organizations](atlas-manage-fed-org-map) to
  your IdP (Identity Provider).

- Created at least one group in your IdP (Identity Provider).

- Add at least one user in your IdP (Identity Provider) application to a group you
  created.

sections.heading: Add Role Mappings in Your Organization and its Projects
sections.body:
- Click Organizations.

  Atlas displays all organizations where you are an
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) in a table.

  - Organizations connected to federated authentication display
     in the Actions column.

  - Organizations unconnected to federated authentication display
    Connect in the Actions column.

- To map roles in an organization:

  - Click Connect to enable federated authentication
    for that organization if needed.

  - Click  and select View.

* Click Manage Role Mappings.

  Atlas displays the Organization Role Mappings
  page.

* Click Create A Role Mapping.

  Atlas displays the Create Role Mapping For Your
  Users page.

At the Map Group and Assign Roles stage:

| Section                   | Action                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Enter Group Name          | Type the name of the group as it is displayed in your IdP (Identity Provider) in this field. Atlas assigns this group to your Atlas role.Note that the name of the group can't exceed 200 characters.If the IdP (Identity Provider) group doesn't exist, you can't enter a new group name to create a new IdP (Identity Provider) group.If you use Microsoft Entra ID as your IdP (Identity Provider) and you selected Group Id as your source attribute, enter the group's Object ID in this field instead of the group's name. To learn more, see Configure Microsoft Entra ID as an Identity Provider. |
| Assign Organization Roles | Click on each Atlas organization role that you want to assign to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |

- If you don't need to assign any Atlas project roles to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish. You can skip the rest of
  this procedure.

- If you need to assign Atlas project roles to this IdP (Identity Provider) group,
  click Next.

The Assign Project Roles stage displays a table. This
table includes project names and the roles you can assign for those
projects. For each project, click the project roles that you want
to assign to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.

- If you don't need to review the roles assigned to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish. You can skip the rest of
  this procedure.

- If you need to review the roles assigned to this IdP (Identity Provider) group,
  click Next.

The Review and Confirm stage displays the organization
and project roles assigned to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.

- If you agree with the roles assigned to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish.

- If you need to change the roles assigned to this IdP (Identity Provider) group, click
   Edit. Atlas returns to the Map
  Group and Assign Roles stage.

sections.heading: Edit Role Mappings in Your Organization and its Projects
sections.body:
- Click Manage Organizations.

  Atlas displays all organizations where you are an
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) in a table.

- Click  next to the desired IdP (Identity Provider)
  Group Name and select View.

* Click Create Role Mappings.

  Atlas displays the Organization Role Mappings
  page.

* Click  Edit to the right of the IdP (Identity Provider) group you
  want to change.

  Atlas displays the Edit Your Role Mapping For This
  Organization page.

At the Map Group and Assign Roles stage:

| Section                   | Action                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Enter Group Name          | Type the name of the group as it is displayed in your IdP (Identity Provider) in this field. Atlas assigns this group to your Atlas role.Note that the name of the group can't exceed 200 characters.If the IdP (Identity Provider) group doesn't exist, you can't enter a new group name to create a new IdP (Identity Provider) group.If you use Microsoft Entra ID as your IdP (Identity Provider) and you selected Group Id as your source attribute, enter the group's Object ID in this field instead of the group's name. To learn more, see Configure Microsoft Entra ID as an Identity Provider. |
| Assign Organization Roles | Click on each Atlas organization role that you want to assign to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |

- If you don't need to assign any Atlas project roles to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish. You can skip the rest of
  this procedure.

- If you need to assign Atlas project roles to this IdP (Identity Provider) group,
  click Next.

The Assign Project Roles stage displays a table. This
table includes project names and the roles you can assign for those
projects. For each project, click the project roles that you want
to assign to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.

- If you don't need to review the roles assigned to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish. You can skip the rest of
  this procedure.

- If you need to review the roles assigned to this IdP (Identity Provider) group,
  click Next.

The Review and Confirm stage displays the organization
and project roles assigned to the IdP (Identity Provider) group.

- If you agree with the roles assigned to this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group, click Finish.

- If you need to change the roles assigned to this IdP (Identity Provider) group, click
   Edit. Atlas returns to the Map
  Group and Assign Roles stage.

sections.heading: Remove One Role Mapping in Your Organization and its Projects
sections.body:
- Click Manage Organizations.

  Atlas displays all organizations where you are an
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) in a table.

  - Organizations connected to federated authentication display
     in the Actions column.

  - Organizations unconnected to federated authentication display
    Connect in the Actions column.

- To map roles in an organization:

  - Click Connect to enable federated authentication
    for that organization if needed.

  - Click  and select View.

* Click Create Role Mappings.

  Atlas displays the Organization Role Mappings
  page.

* Click  Delete to the right of the IdP (Identity Provider) group you
  want to remove.

  Atlas displays the
  Delete role mappings for this group modal.

* Click Delete to remove all role mappings from this
  IdP (Identity Provider) group.

  If you don't want to remove all role mappings, click
  Cancel.
